title: AIGX Genome — canonical JSON data model (v1.1)
description: The canonical JSON representation of an AIGX genome, as produced by a conforming parser or by `aigx lint --format json` / `aigx-lint --format json`. AIGX files on disk are XML-style text (see AIGX-1.1.abnf); this schema describes the DATA MODEL a tool extracts from them, not the on-disk syntax. Validating a genome against this schema is OPTIONAL and is not a condition of conformance (conformance is defined in AIGX-1.1.md §13–§15).

aigxVersion.description: The AIGX specification version this model targets, e.g. "1.1".

root.description: Absolute or repo-relative path of the repository root the genomes were resolved against.

genomes.description: One entry per discovered .aigx/ directory. A single-element array is the common (non-monorepo) case; multiple elements model a hierarchical/sharded layout (AIGX-1.1.md §5.2).

dir.description: Repo-relative path of this genome's .aigx/ directory.

rules.description: All rules declared in this genome's concern files.

files.description: All <file> entries in this genome's files.aigx (the per-file boundary index).

domainCards.description: Parsed domain cards (<key>.aigx) colocated with source folders.

id.description: Stable rule identifier PREFIX-SLUG (AIGX-1.1.md §9). MUST be unique within a genome.

concern.description: The concern this rule belongs to, derived from the <aigx-CONCERN> root element (e.g. "architecture").

path.description: Repo-root-relative path of the file (AIGX-1.1.md §5.2, §10.1). MUST NOT escape the repository root.

domain.description: The domain/feature key this file belongs to (optional).

role.description: One line: what this file is for.

checks.description: Rule ids the consumer MUST verify before finishing an edit to this file. Each MUST resolve to a rule id in the applicable genome (validator rule V2).

boundary.description: A <forbid> or <gotcha>: prose plus an optional salience level.

priority.description: The pri attribute. The validated uniform level is "CRIT"; null means normal priority (AIGX-1.1.md §10.3).

path.description: The source folder this card describes.

ruleId.description: The rule id this fact enforces, if tagged.
